metadata.title: Oil geopolitics + precious-metals backwardation (the physical-market stress signal)
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (EIA STEO, IEA Oil Market Reports, JPMorgan/Goldman, BullionStar, Investing.com/BofA, Andrew Maguire/Sprott, Seeking Alpha). Note: several precious-metals sources are bullion-dealer/analyst commentary - flagged as such.
metadata.frame: Two simultaneous signals: (1) OIL whipsawed by geopolitics (Hormuz/Iran, Russia sanctions) on top of structural oversupply; (2) SILVER/GOLD BACKWARDATION = acute physical tightness and a 'paper vs physical' dislocation often read as forced/institutional pressure. Both feed the macro-rates and trust backdrop the AI bubble sits on.

geopolitics.fact: De facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz from ~Feb 28 2026 (military action); ~20% of global oil supply transits Hormuz. Drove a price spike.
~70% of Russian crude now under restrictions; flows redirected to China, Indian flows declining - a structural reshuffle of global crude trade.

price_action.note_spike: Brent averaged ~$117 in April 2026 (highest since Jun 2022) on Hormuz disruption
price_action.note_crash: then fell to lowest since Feb 2021 on oversupply + weak demand

oil.read: Extreme two-way volatility: a geopolitical supply shock (Iran/Hormuz, Russia) superimposed on OPEC+ oversupply and soft demand. Spiking oil = inflation/rates pressure (re-widens bank HTM losses, tightens the carry trade); crashing oil = demand-weakness signal. Either way it injects instability into the rate path the AI-debt complex depends on.

floor_model.summary: Model the DEFENDED price floor (#75): the band below which producers cut supply / OPEC+ defends, given the offsetting flow LOSSES (Iran/Hormuz, sanctioned Russia) against a structural 2026 surplus. The floor is a band, not a number; the inputs are fact, the band is a labeled estimate.
floor_model.break_evens: Fiscal break-evens (the price a state needs to balance its budget) sit well ABOVE operating break-evens. Saudi fiscal break-even ~$85+/bbl Brent (IMF); Russia ~$70-90 fiscal; US shale OPERATING (cash) break-even ~$30-40 but new-well full-cycle ~$55-65 WTI - below ~$60 WTI US shale growth stalls (rig drop), which is the market-clearing floor mechanism.
floor_model.supply_loss_offsets: Bullish floor supports: ~70% of Russian crude under restriction (Rosneft/Lukoil sanctioned Oct-22-2025) redirecting flows at a discount; de-facto Hormuz disruption (~20% of seaborne oil at risk); OPEC+ retains spare capacity it can withhold. These remove/threaten barrels and put a floor under price.
floor_model.surplus_overhang: Bearish ceiling pressure: a structural ~3.8-4 mb/d 2026 surplus (OPEC+ unwinding cuts + non-OPEC growth) + soft China/EU demand - the reason the curve whipsawed toward contango and forecasters cluster Brent ~$60-65 for 2026.
floor_model.defended_band: Net: a producer-/OPEC+-defended floor of ~$60-70 Brent (central ~$65), enforced by US-shale rig response below ~$60 WTI and OEC+ supply management; with a fat UPSIDE tail to $100-130+ on any actual Hormuz closure (the Apr-2026 ~$117 spike is the proof of the tail). The asymmetry: limited downside (defended), large geopolitical upside.
floor_model.grade: break-evens + sanction/surplus inputs FACT; the ~$60-70 defended band is a labeled estimate (date/level not forecastable - cf. spec-unwind-timing)

precious_metals_backwardation.signal: BACKWARDATION (spot > futures) in silver/gold = the market paying a premium for METAL NOW vs paper claims later - the classic signature of a physical squeeze / distrust of paper settlement.

data.value: London trading ~$1.55 OVER New York COMEX
collapsed to ~0.156c/oz from 64c two weeks earlier
data.normal: tightening EFP coincides with vault outflows / delivery stress

four_forces: a physical run on paper silver (delivery demand)
precious_metals_backwardation.read: Institutional traders taking COMEX delivery 'because it's the only accessible source' and a >30x lease-rate spike are forced-pressure / squeeze conditions. Consistent with commodities-metals.json (silver spiked toward $90-121 then pulled back to ~$67). The 'paper vs physical' fracture is the commodity-market analogue of the financial bubble's RPO-vs-cash gap: claims vastly exceed deliverable substance.
precious_metals_backwardation.caveat: Much silver-squeeze commentary comes from bullion dealers/permabull analysts; the lease-rate spike, EFP collapse, and London-COMEX gap are the hard, corroborated facts. Treat triple-digit price predictions as opinion.

linkage_to_bubble: Both the oil whipsaw and the precious-metals backwardation are stress fractures in the PHYSICAL economy that bound the AI/financial bubble: (1) they drive the rates/inflation path that governs bank HTM losses (macro-fdic.json) and the carry-trade unwind (macro-carry-trades.json); (2) the 'paper >> physical' dislocation in metals rhymes structurally with 'RPO/commitments >> cash' in the AI core (data/graph.json) and 'off-balance-sheet claims >> real receivables' in First Brands. The same pattern - promises far exceeding deliverable substance - appears in commodities, in credit, and in AI compute simultaneously.

edges.raw_instrument: de-facto Strait of Hormuz closure (Feb-2026 conflict); ~20% of seaborne oil transits
edges.notes: the upside-tail driver - the Apr-2026 ~$117 spike

edges.raw_instrument: sanctioned Russian crude (Rosneft/Lukoil, Oct-22-2025) redirected to China/India at a discount
edges.notes: ~70% of Russian crude restricted - a floor support via removed barrels

edges.raw_instrument: OPEC+ production policy defends a ~$60-70 Brent floor (fiscal break-even ~$85)
edges.notes: the defended-floor mechanism modeled in oil.floor_model
